data.title: Axogen names Lindsey Hartley as finance chief
data.description:
• Axogen (NASDAQ:AXGN) said it has appointed Lindsey Hartley as its chief financial officer, effective May 12.
  • Hartley currently serves as vice president and corporate controller at Axogen and will succeed Nir Naor.
  • Naor will remain with the company in an advisory capacity until July 1.
  • Hartley has more than 19 years of financial leadership experience and had joined the firm in October 2021.

data.title: Hims & Hers Health to offer $450M convertible senior notes
data.description:
Hims & Hers Health (NYSE:HIMS) plans to offer $450M aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering.

The notes will accrue interest payable semi-annually in arrears and will mature on May 15, 2030, unless earlier repurchased, redeemed or converted.

The interest rate, initial conversion rate and other terms of the notes will be determined at the pricing of the offering.

The company also expects to grant the initial purchasers an option to purchase, for settlement within a period of 13 days from, and including, the date the notes are first issued, up to an additional $67.5M of notes.

Hims & Hers (NYSE:HIMS) intends to use proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including accelerating global expansion through both organic growth and strategic acquisitions.

Shares were down 3.95% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Black Diamond Group to conduct normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Black Diamond Group (OTCQX:BDIMF) said the Toronto Stock Exchange has approved its intention to renew its normal course issuer bid.
  • The bid will start on May 12, and in the 12-month period, the firm can purchase up to 4.5M shares.

data.title: Papa John's gains after earnings topper, reiterated full-year sale guidance
data.description:
Papa John's International (NASDAQ:PZZA) gained in early trading on Thursday after turning in a better-than-feared earnings report in the face of consumer discretionary spending headwinds.

North America comparable sales were down 2.7% year-over-year to match the consensus expectation. Comparable sales for domestic company-owned restaurants were down 5% and were down 2% for North America franchised restaurants. International comparable sales were up 3% during the quarter.

Global system-wide restaurant sales were up 1% to $1.22 billion, as higher international comparable sales and trailing twelve-month net restaurant growth were partially offset by lower North American comparable sales.

Adjusted EBITDA fell to $49.6 million from $60.6 million a year ago. EPS came in at $0.36 vs. $0.35 consensus and $0.67 a year ago. Higher G&A expenses related to incremental marketing and loyalty investments to support the company’s brand positioning work and the biannual franchisee conference were a drag on profits during the quarter.

"Our strategic investments in marketing and technology are driving early momentum in the business, and customers are responding positively to our strengthened value proposition and enhanced digital and loyalty experiences, as evidenced by sequential improvement in comparable sales and transactions," stated CEO Todd Penegor.

Looking ahead, the pizza chain operator reiterated its full-year guidance for system-wide sales to be up 2% to 5%. Adjusted EBITDA of $200 million to $220 million is anticipated for the full year.

Shares of Papa John's (NASDAQ:PZZA) were up 2.1% in premarket action to cut into the 19% year-to-date decline.

data.title: Newell Brands to offer $1 billion of senior notes
data.description:
• Newell Brands (NASDAQ:NWL) said it is looking to offer $1 billion aggregate principal amount of senior unsecured notes due 2028 in a private offering.
  • The firm said it expects to use the net proceeds, along with cash on hand, to redeem $1 billion aggregate principal amount of its outstanding 4.200% senior notes due 2026.
  • The proceeds will also be used to pay related fees and expenses in connection with the offering and the redemption.

data.title: Dave stock soars after massive Q1 earnings beat, guidance boost
data.description:
Dave (NASDAQ:DAVE) stock leapt 27% in Thursday premarket trading after the neobank and fintech ratcheted up 2025 guidance as its Q1 top and bottom lines blew away the average analyst estimates. Its membership grew and originations of its ExtraCash short-term loans jumped 46%.

The company now expects full-year 2025 GAAP net operating revenue of  $460M-$475M (vs. $421.8M consensus), up from its previous guidance of $415M-$435M. It raised guidance for adjusted EBITDA to $155M-$165M from its prior range of $110M-$120M.

Q1 adjusted EPS of $2.48, crushing the average analyst estimate of $0.76, surged from $0.62 in last year's Q1.

Q1 total net operating revenue of $108.0M, topping the consensus of $93.6M, grew from $73.6M in the year-ago quarter.

"Revenue grew at the fastest year-over-year pace since 2021 when our business was a fraction of its current size," said Founder and CEO Jason Wilk. "Given the operating leverage of our business model, adjusted EBITDA increased 235% year-over-year and 32% sequentially to $44.2 M." 

The acceleration was driven by execution across its business. In addition, its new fee structure enhanced monetization and conversion rates while maintaining strong member retention, he said.

ExtraCash originations rose 46% to $1.5B, and the average 28-day delinquency rate improved 33 basis points to 1.50%. Dave Debit Card spend climbed 24% Y/Y to $488M.

The company acquired 569K new members in the quarter, compared with 766K in the previous quarter and 566K members in last year's Q1. Total members rose to 12.4M from 12.1M in the previous quarter and 10.8M a year ago.

Average ExtraCash size rose to $192 from $179 in Q4 2024 and $159 in Q1 2024. Average revenue per ExtraCash grew to $11.4 from $10.1 in the previous quarter and $9.0 a year ago.

Conference call at 8:30 AM ET.

Earlier, Dave GAAP EPS of $1.97 beats by $1.21, revenue of $108M beats by $14.4M

data.title: Alzamend Neuro to conduct reverse stock split
data.description:
• Alzamend Neuro (NASDAQ:ALZN) said its board has approved a one-for-nine reverse stock split of the firm's common stock.
  • The split is a part of the firm's plan to maintain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.

data.title: MannKind Non-GAAP EPS of $0.07 beats by $0.01, revenue of $78.35M beats by $2.49M
data.description:
• MannKind press release (NASDAQ:MNKD): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.07 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $78.35M (+18.2% Y/Y) beats by $2.49M.
  • Cash, cash equivalents and investments as of March 31, 2025 totaled $198 million.
